metadataSource.releaseSelector: latest non-draft, non-prerelease release whose tag starts with browser-v

knownIssues: Live browser-extension smoke covers self-hosted environment selection, password login, initial sync, account profile reads, and empty-vault render against a local synthetic account only.
The browser run used Brave's Chromium extension host because the installed branded Chrome build did not expose the unpacked extension under the local evidence flags.
The staging login-with-device run used an isolated Chrome for Testing host with unsafe extension debugging enabled only for the pinned unpacked test extension.
TOTP login has official CLI plus HTTP auth lifecycle evidence only; no browser-extension TOTP evidence is recorded yet.
Encrypted device key and bulk trust update have fixture coverage only; no live client evidence is recorded yet.
Account-key read and one-time initialization have fixture and local synthetic lifecycle evidence only; no official-client or production activation evidence is recorded.
Feature surfaces outside the initial personal-vault scope are expected to fail explicitly or remain unsupported.

metadataSource.releaseSelector: latest non-draft, non-prerelease release whose tag starts with desktop-v

knownIssues: On 2026-07-13, official Desktop 2026.6.1 exposed a server routing defect: permanent delete via DELETE /api/ciphers/:id returned an opaque 404 for a trashed cipher; this change corrects that route and adds the upstream POST /api/ciphers/:id/delete alias. A post-fix official-client lifecycle rerun is not recorded yet.
Live Desktop smoke covers self-hosted staging selection, password login, initial sync, pending login notification, approval, and empty-vault rendering with a synthetic account only.
The live run used the official ACCESS_TOKEN_LOCATION=DISK switch and an isolated Electron user-data directory; it did not patch the official app or modify a normal official-client Keychain entry.
Item create, update, delete, logout/login persistence, and a full live regression remain unverified for Desktop.
TOTP login has official CLI plus HTTP auth lifecycle evidence only; no desktop TOTP evidence is recorded yet.
Encrypted device key and bulk trust update have fixture coverage only; no live client evidence is recorded yet.
Account-key read and one-time initialization have fixture and local synthetic lifecycle evidence only; no official-client or production activation evidence is recorded.
Feature surfaces outside the initial personal-vault scope are expected to fail explicitly or remain unsupported.

metadataSource.releaseSelector: latest non-draft, non-prerelease Password Manager release whose tag ends with -bwpm

knownIssues: Live Android smoke covers official F-Droid APK self-hosted environment selection, password login, first empty-vault sync persistence, billing subscription read, and config refresh against a local synthetic account only.
The smoke exposed official Retrofit rejection of SQLite CURRENT_TIMESTAMP values; HonoWarden now normalizes outward API timestamps to UTC ISO-8601 before the passing clean run.
TOTP login has official CLI plus HTTP auth lifecycle evidence only; no Android TOTP evidence is recorded yet.
Encrypted device key and bulk trust update have fixture coverage only; no live client evidence is recorded yet.
Account-key read and one-time initialization have fixture and local synthetic lifecycle evidence only; no official-client or production activation evidence is recorded.
Feature surfaces outside the initial personal-vault scope are expected to fail explicitly or remain unsupported.

metadataSource.releaseSelector: latest non-draft, non-prerelease release whose tag starts with cli-v

knownIssues: Live CLI smoke covers config, password login, sync, one synthetic login item create/update/trash/permanent-delete flow, account revision lookup, official CLI one-step TOTP login, refresh grant, and HTTP auth lifecycle recent-auth guards.
The live smoke used a local compression-stripping HTTPS proxy because wrangler dev returned gzip chunked responses that the tracked CLI HTTP stack rejected.

TOTP setup, change, disable, and revoke-all-other-sessions evidence uses direct HTTP routes with CLI-issued or recent password-authenticated tokens because the tracked CLI does not expose those account-management flows in this scope.
Encrypted device key and bulk trust update have fixture coverage only; no live client evidence is recorded yet.
Account-key read and one-time initialization have fixture and local synthetic lifecycle evidence only; no official-client or production activation evidence is recorded.
Feature surfaces outside the initial personal-vault scope are expected to fail explicitly or remain unsupported.
